response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong technical breadth, proven leadership capabilities, and solid infrastructure experience, they fail to meet the fundamental minimum requirement: 8 years of C++ programming experience. The resume shows less than 1 year of professional C++ work (2014-2015), making them poorly equipped to lead technical strategy for a C++-heavy cloud compute platform. The role requires deep C++ expertise paired with cloud architecture leadership, neither of which are adequately demonstrated.
strengths: Strong testing and TDD culture champion across multiple organizations, with formal workshop creation and training curriculum development
Proven technical leadership through staff-level roles, mentoring programs (code katas, refactoring dojos), and course instruction
Substantial large-scale distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby with microsystems handling millions of requests/second and infrastructure tooling expertise (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS CDK)
concerns: Critical C++ deficiency: role requires 8 years but candidate demonstrates less than 1 year professionally, with most recent C++ work ending in 2015
No explicit production cloud compute platform architecture experience; infrastructure work has been in secondary languages (DLang, TypeScript)
Leadership experience is generalist software engineering rather than specialized cloud/DevOps technical mentorship with cloud platform depth

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (8 years required vs <1 demonstrated)
Technical mentoring and leadership track record
